message:
fix(session_picker): skip re-parsing unchanged session files and reduce test flakiness

The session picker previously re-parsed every on-disk session file each
time `list_sessions_for_project` was called, even when the index already
held up-to-date metadata. On projects with many sessions this added
unnecessary I/O and latency.

Now `scan_sessions_on_disk` accepts the existing cached-by-path map and
compares each file's last-modified timestamp and size against the cached
row. Files whose stats match are skipped entirely, avoiding redundant
JSON parsing. When a file has changed, the freshly parsed metadata is
written back to the index via the new `upsert_session_meta` public
method so subsequent calls benefit from the cache.

Also restructures the retain/scan/merge flow: missing paths are pruned
first, then disk scanning runs only on uncached or changed files, and
failed paths are cleaned from both the index and the in-memory map.

Removes the now-unused `HashSet` import from session_picker.

Additionally fixes a flaky test in `agent.rs` where
`submit_continue_uses_session_deadline` could receive an unrelated probe
message before the expected one. The test now loops on `recv_timeout`
until the expected deadline value arrives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
